function.name: supabase_column_type_mapper
function.description: Map a PostgreSQL column type to its TypeScript equivalent.

meta.jsBody: function supabase_column_type_mapper(args) { var pgType = String(args.postgresType || '').toLowerCase(); var map = { 'text': 'string', 'varchar': 'string', 'char': 'string', 'uuid': 'string', 'int2': 'number', 'int4': 'number', 'int8': 'number', 'float4': 'number', 'float8': 'number', 'numeric': 'number', 'integer': 'number', 'bigint': 'number', 'smallint': 'number', 'real': 'number', 'double precision': 'number', 'bool': 'boolean', 'boolean': 'boolean', 'json': 'Record<string, unknown>', 'jsonb': 'Record<string, unknown>', 'timestamp': 'string', 'timestamptz': 'string', 'date': 'string', 'time': 'string', 'bytea': 'string' }; return { tsType: map[pgType] || 'unknown' }; }

trajectories.userPrompt: What TypeScript type maps to PostgreSQL text?

function.name: supabase_connection_string_parser
function.description: Parse a PostgreSQL connection string into host, port, database, and user components. Pure regex, no network.

meta.jsBody: function supabase_connection_string_parser(args) { var url = String(args.url || ''); var m = /^postgres(?:ql)?:\/\/([^:@]+)(?::([^@]*))?@([^:\/]+)(?::(\d+))?\/(.+)$/.exec(url); if (!m) return { host: '', port: 5432, database: '', user: '' }; return { host: m[3] || '', port: m[4] ? Number(m[4]) : 5432, database: m[5] || '', user: m[1] || '' }; }

function.name: supabase_rls_policy_template
function.description: Emit a Supabase RLS policy DDL template for a given table, role, and operation.

meta.jsBody: function supabase_rls_policy_template(args) { var op = String(args.operation || '').toUpperCase(); var role = String(args.role || 'authenticated'); var table = String(args.tableName || ''); var policy = 'CREATE POLICY "' + table + '_' + op.toLowerCase() + '_' + role + '" ON public.' + table + ' FOR ' + op + ' TO ' + role + ' USING (auth.uid() = user_id);'; return { policy: policy }; }

trajectories.userPrompt: Write an RLS policy for select on profiles for authenticated users.

result.policy: CREATE POLICY "profiles_select_authenticated" ON public.profiles FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
